tasks_completed.description: M1 — Hash chain trailer emission at git commit-tree time
tasks_completed.location: hooks/commit.sh inside run_private_index_commit, just before git commit-tree call (~L2050-2128)
tasks_completed.rationale: Per ticket M1 — 6 cycle artifacts hashed (SHA-256, 64-hex), task-flow ordered, omit-not-zero on missing artifacts, git interpret-trailers --in-place --if-exists replace --if-missing add with canonical POSIX pre-strip regex, CLAUDE_COMMIT_SKIP_HASH_TRAILERS=1 operator override (trailer emission disabled; ARTIFACT_SHA256_JSON still computed for audit JSON forensic value per ticket line 97 + codex round 5 blocker fix), absolute temp path under ${CLAUDE_TMPDIR:-/tmp}, bridge mode exempt by construction.

tasks_completed.location: hooks/commit.sh closed-task path, after closure-evidence detection, before COMMIT_MSG/run_private_index_commit invocation (~L2604-2740)
tasks_completed.rationale: Per ticket M2 — 8 anchored regex patterns scan subject+body of CALLER_MESSAGE; four-sentinel qa-report.qa.status parse (__absent__/__invalid__/__unknown__/fail → REFUSE; pass → ACCEPT; warning → ACCEPT iff same-paragraph qualifier). Skip evaluation order MANDATORY: bridge → force → force-rescue → force-manifest → env → MESSAGE_SOURCE==auto → caller-active. Force-mode skip-value branches set additionally inside run_private_index_commit so audit JSON emits message_guard for force paths (codex round 5 blocker 2 fix — force dispatch at L2366-2395 short-circuits before reaching M2 location).

tasks_completed.location: hooks/commit.sh end of argv-parse block, after S-FORCE-WARNING (~L416-446)
tasks_completed.rationale: Per ticket M3 — case-sensitive type whitelist regex ^(feat|fix|refactor|docs|test|chore|build|ci|perf|style|revert)(\([^)]+\))?(!)?:\s+\S.*$. Universal application (MESSAGE_SOURCE ∈ {caller, auto}; F8 reversal preserved). Skip evaluation: bridge → env → apply. Refusal: stderr + exit 2 only, no audit JSON. Fires before closure detection so non-CC bad subject exits early without expensive work.

tasks_completed.description: S1 — Audit log persistence preflight inside run_private_index_commit
tasks_completed.location: hooks/commit.sh L134 (CLAUDE_LOG_DIR default change) + L1469-1505 (single-chokepoint preflight inside run_private_index_commit)
tasks_completed.rationale: Per ticket S1 + iter-2 B1 — default changed from ${CLAUDE_HOME}/logs (tmpfs in this env) to hard-coded /var/lib/claude/commit-audit (FHS-conformant, ext4-backed). Preflight: readlink -f → mkdir -p -m 700 → re-readlink → df -T → refuse tmpfs unless CLAUDE_AUDIT_PERSIST_DISABLED=1 → write-probe → return 2 on failure. Single chokepoint covers ALL 6 invocation paths (closed-task PRIMARY/SECONDARY/manifest at :2613/:2613/:2610, force/force-rescue/force-manifest at :2394/:2391/:2385). Bridge mode exempt by construction (does not call function). Sets AUDIT_PERSIST_VALUE for audit JSON when operator-acknowledged tmpfs is in play.

Per iter-2 B2 — sanitize derived scope via scope = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9_-]', '', stem.replace('_','-'))[:24] or 'task' BEFORE substitution into the subject string. Closes F8 construction-hole: M3's \([^)]+\) regex now accepts helper-emitted subjects for any filename (e.g., basenames with `)`, `(`, `.`, whitespace). Empty-post-sanitization falls through to 'task' via Python truthiness on or-chain.

tasks_completed.description: Audit JSON extended with new M1/M2/M3/S1 fields
tasks_completed.rationale: Audit-JSON heredoc invocation extended with 4 new positional args (ARTIFACT_SHA256_JSON, AUDIT_PERSIST_VALUE, M2_MESSAGE_GUARD_RESULT, M3_TYPE_LINT_RESULT) and fields conditionally added to data dict so the per-commit audit record now includes artifact_sha256 (DOC-14 mirror), message_guard (DOC-15), type_lint (DOC-16), and audit_persist (DOC-17 when set).

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: 640a427d59 - baseline; no regression. 4 enterprise hardening items + B2 helper sanitization added to the surface that codex audited on 2026-05-11.
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: M1/M3 were never specified in the original commit.sh requirements. M2 (LLM-class hallucination) is a modern audit concern. S1 (tmpfs default) inherited from CLAUDE_HOME convention that resolved through symlink to tmpfs in this environment. B2 (helper scope sanitization) closes a construction-hole exposed by F8 in iter-1 codex round 2.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: M1 adds tamper-evidence via SHA-256 trailers (file-on-disk-at-commit-tree-time bytes). M2 refuses verification claims not backed by qa-report. M3 enforces CC type discipline. S1 hard-defaults to FHS-conformant /var/lib/claude/commit-audit with tmpfs-refusal preflight. B2 ensures helper-emitted subjects pass M3 for any filename.

diff_stats.lines_added: approximately 470 across the 4 files (commit.sh +280, commit.md +175, commit-smoke.sh +160, auto-commit-message.sh +9)
diff_stats.lines_removed: approximately 5 (replacements of CLAUDE_LOG_DIR default and the helper scope derivation line)
new_symbols_introduced: Bash function-scope locals: AUDIT_PERSIST_VALUE, ARTIFACT_SHA256_JSON, COMMIT_MSG_FOR_TREE, M1_TRAILER_FILE, M1_HASH_OUTPUT, M1_TRAILER_LINES, M1_AUDIT_MAP, M1_TRAILER_ARGS, M2_MESSAGE_GUARD_RESULT, M2_GUARD_OUTPUT, M2_MATCHED, M2_STATUS, M3_TYPE_LINT_RESULT, M3_SUBJECT, M3_MATCHED_TYPE, M3_SUBJECT_PREVIEW
No new Python module-level symbols (only heredoc-local)
diff_stats.minimum_possible_lines_estimate: approximately 400 — the 4 enterprise items each require a non-trivial regex/parse/skip-matrix block + DOC sections; helper sanitization is the only single-line surgical change
diff_stats.justification_for_overage: The 4 enterprise items + 4 DOC sections + 3 smoke paths are independently mandated by ticket. M1 trailer mechanic (pre-strip + interpret-trailers + audit mirror) is irreducible. M2 8-pattern regex + four-sentinel parse + paragraph-proximity rule for warning status cannot compress further. S1 single-chokepoint preflight covers 6 invocation paths in one location (codex-confirmed). M3 is the smallest of the four (~30 lines). DOC sections are required verbatim by AC8. Smoke paths are required by AC6. Codex round 5 unconditional-audit-hash fix added defensive value computation outside the trailer-emit gate. Codex round 5 force-mode-skip fix added a 14-line case statement inside run_private_index_commit. None of this is over-engineering — every block maps 1:1 to an AC clause.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex round 6 returned 2 in_scope_real_bug blockers + 6 confirmed-clean Q-answers. Blocker 1: M1 ARTIFACT_SHA256_JSON gated entirely behind CLAUDE_COMMIT_SKIP_HASH_TRAILERS != 1, violating ticket line 97 contract that the env override disables trailer emission ONLY while audit JSON still records artifact_sha256. Blocker 2: M2 force-mode skip-value branches are defensively unreachable (force dispatch short-circuits at :2366-2395 before M2 location), so force / force-rescue / force-manifest commits emit audit JSON without the `message_guard: skipped (...)` field that DOC-15 + AC2 require. Q1 (M1 quoting), Q2 (M3 ordering), Q4 (S1 ordering), Q5 (B2 sufficiency), Q6 (R5 handling) all confirmed clean; Q3 (M2 defensive branches) clean for forward-compat but secondary issue surfaced.

in_scope_real_bug: Blocker 1 — M1 ARTIFACT_SHA256_JSON computation must be unconditional (per ticket §M1 operator-override line 97)
Blocker 2 — M2 force-mode message_guard audit values not emitted because force dispatch short-circuits before M2 location
confirmed_clean: Q1 — M1 trailer Token=Value syntax with array-quoted args and single-quoted sed: no escaping risk
Q2 — M3 ordering correct (MESSAGE_SOURCE set at L396 before M3 at L416)
Q3 — M2 force defensive branches OK to keep (forward-compat) but separate audit-emission issue exposed
Q5 — B2 sanitization sufficient (ctype whitelist + scope safe-set + or-'task' fallback)
Q6 — R5-1 inline DOC-16 fix correct; R5-2 ticket residual out of dev edit scope

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both in_scope_real_bug blockers fixed in-stream BEFORE dev-report. Fix 1: ARTIFACT_SHA256_JSON computation moved outside the CLAUDE_COMMIT_SKIP_HASH_TRAILERS gate at commit.sh:2050-2080; only the interpret-trailers + COMMIT_MSG_FOR_TREE mutation remains gated. Fix 2: added a case statement inside run_private_index_commit at L1486-1500 (after S1 preflight, before tmp_dir declaration) that sets M2_MESSAGE_GUARD_RESULT='skipped (force|force-rescue|force-manifest)' based on the function's mode argument when M2_MESSAGE_GUARD_RESULT is empty. Both fixes verified via re-run of commit-smoke.sh (25/25 PASS) + bash -n + python ast.parse on all 29 heredoc blocks.
codex_consult.verdict_drove: Dev cycle proceeds to dev-report with 2 codex-flagged blockers resolved in-stream. Remaining open items (R5-2 ticket residual) are documented as out-of-dev-edit-scope and noted in ba_qa_leftovers_handled.

self_verification.notes: bash -n /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/hooks/commit.sh: clean. Python ast.parse on all 29 embedded heredoc blocks: clean. bash scripts/commit-smoke.sh end-to-end: pass=25 fail=0 skipped=0 (22 prior + 3 new + 2 sub-assertions for path l). Direct regex tests on M3 whitelist (7 test cases) and M2 pattern set (10 test cases) all pass.

recommendations: Operators currently relying on the legacy ${CLAUDE_HOME}/logs audit-log path (tmpfs in this environment) must either accept the new /var/lib/claude/commit-audit hard-default or explicitly set CLAUDE_LOG_DIR to a non-tmpfs persistent path. The wrapper will refuse-with-exit-2 on tmpfs unless CLAUDE_AUDIT_PERSIST_DISABLED=1 opt-in.

Verifiers downstream of a commit should rehash artifacts ON DISK at the time of verification, not via git show <commit>:<path>. The trailer reflects file-on-disk-at-commit-tree-time bytes, not the tree-blob. DOC-14 documents this verbatim.

If git interpret-trailers is unavailable in some operator's git binary (git < 2.32), the M1 trailer emission silently no-ops on that invocation; the audit JSON still records artifact_sha256 so forensic value is preserved. A future cycle could add a hand-rolled trailer fallback if telemetry shows this gap matters.
